rationale_for_baseline_tier: A drowsy, obtunded patient with profound flaccid weakness, intractable vomiting with aspiration risk, palpitations/irregular pulse and marked dehydration from a severe electrolyte/metabolic derangement is an immediate threat to life (impending coma and fatal arrhythmia). The patient is bedbound and cannot self-transport, and requires airway protection, IV rehydration and cardiac monitoring en route — an emergency ambulance (T5, call 000) is the only appropriate tier, consistent with the source note having activated 000. The patient-reportable time-critical features (reduced consciousness, inability to move, ongoing vomiting) justify T5 directly, not merely a telehealth precaution.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Baseline presentation — obtundation with profound weakness, vomiting and palpitations from a suspected severe electrolyte emergency
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Call 000 for an emergency ambulance immediately — minutes matter
escalation_edges.rationale: The whole picture is a life-threatening metabolic emergency in a patient who cannot self-transport; an ambulance provides airway support, IV fluids and cardiac monitoring.
escalation_edges.communication_template: This is an emergency — call 000 for an ambulance right now and tell them he is drowsy, very weak, vomiting and his heart is racing. Do not try to drive him in yourselves.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Collapse, no pulse, or the heart clearly stopping
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Start CPR immediately and keep 000 on the line
escalation_edges.rationale: Electrolyte derangement can trigger a fatal arrhythmia and cardiac arrest; immediate CPR and paramedic resuscitation are the critical resource.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If he stops breathing or you can't feel a pulse, roll him onto his back, start CPR, and tell 000 straight away — they will talk you through it.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Becomes completely unrousable / deepening drowsiness before the ambulance arrives
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Keep him in the recovery position and stay on the line with 000
escalation_edges.rationale: Deepening obtunded consciousness signals progression toward coma and loss of airway protection; recovery position and continued emergency care are essential.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If he becomes impossible to wake, keep him on his side in the recovery position, make sure his airway is clear, and tell 000 he is getting worse.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Vomiting while drowsy — risk of choking/aspiration; or a seizure
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Keep him on his side, give nothing by mouth, and call 000 back if not already on the line
escalation_edges.rationale: A drowsy patient with paralytic ileus who vomits is at high risk of aspiration; a seizure signals severe derangement — both need airway protection and emergency care.
escalation_edges.communication_template: Keep him lying on his side and do not give him anything to eat or drink. If he vomits, clear his mouth. If he has a seizure, protect his head and tell 000.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Regular thiazide diuretic plus OTC calcium supplements — likely potentiating drivers of the hypercalcaemia
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Advise withholding both immediately and bringing all medication and supplement bottles to hospital so the team can identify and reverse the cause.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Spouse is the sole person present and is managing a heavy, obtunded, vomiting patient alone
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Give clear, simple, one-step-at-a-time instructions, keep the carer on the line, and have them unlock the door for paramedics; do not assume they can lift or reposition the patient unaided.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Call 000 for an ambulance right now and say he is drowsy, very weak, vomiting and his heart is racing. While you wait: roll him onto his side in the recovery position to keep his airway clear, give him NOTHING to eat or drink, stop his water tablet and calcium tablets, and gather all his medication bottles for the paramedics. Stay with him and watch his breathing.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Call 000 immediately for an emergency ambulance
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000 operates 24 hours a day — this is an emergency and must not wait for clinic hours or a GP call-back.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: The hospital will provide a discharge summary with the confirmed electrolyte results and cause; take this to the GP to review the thiazide and calcium/vitamin D supplement use and arrange endocrine follow-up.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating this as gastroenteritis, dehydration or a non-urgent electrolyte problem and advising oral fluids, GP review or watchful waiting instead of an emergency ambulance.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Progression to hypercalcaemic coma, aspiration, or a fatal arrhythmia while awaiting a lower level of care.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Recognising the emergency but advising self-transport to ED rather than a 000 ambulance for an obtunded, unstable, non-ambulant patient; unmonitored deterioration or aspiration during an unsupported car journey.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Over-triage is not a meaningful risk here — T5 is the correct and highest tier.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: The specific offending cation (calcium vs sodium vs potassium) and its exact level cannot be determined without hospital bloods.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Escalate on the emergency pattern regardless of the exact electrolyte — the management (000, airway protection, nil-by-mouth, withhold contributing drugs) is the same, and hospital bloods will identify the cause.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Delaying escalation to try to pin the exact electrolyte remotely, or reassuring the family because no specific number is available.
